name: Cranberry and Orange Charlotte
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely grate the rind from 1 orange and squeeze the juice. Peel and segment the other orange, discarding all white pith.
2. Put the cranberries and sugar in a saucepan with 150 ml water. Add the grated orange rind and crushed cardamom seeds. Heat gently until the sugar is dissolved, then simmer for 10 minutes. Taste for sweetness; add more sugar if necessary.
3. Transfer half of the mixture to a blender or food processor and work until smooth. Press through a sieve into a bowl. Stir in the orange juice to make a coulis; set aside.
4. Add the orange segments to the other half of the cranberry mixture.
5. Assemble the charlottes using 4 individual round tins or pudding molds, about 200 ml in capacity. Cut thin slices from the brioche loaf and cut 8 circles to fit the base and top of the molds. Place 4 brioche circles in melted butter at the bottom of the tins.
6. Cut thin strips of brioche, dip in butter, and line the sides of the tins. Fill with the cranberry and orange segment mixture. Dip the remaining brioche circles in butter and place on top.
7. Place on a baking sheet and bake in a preheated oven at 200°C or 400°F for 25 minutes, or until crisp and well browned. Remove carefully from the tins.
8. Transfer to individual plates and dust with icing sugar. Surround with the cranberry and orange coulis, dot with fromage frais, and garnish with orange rind shreds. Serve immediately.
